expo.name: TrustTrace
expo._userInterfaceStyle_note: The palette is deliberately light-only for v1 (all screens hardcode a light theme); declaring it here keeps the OS from rendering system surfaces dark against light content. A dark palette is a post-1.0 item.

android._permissionsNote: Only permissions backed by a shipped implementation are declared. INTERNET is added automatically by RN for the opt-in cloud/backend calls. RECEIVE_SMS/BIND_ACCESSIBILITY_SERVICE/SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW were removed until their native services exist (Play Store rejects unbacked sensitive permissions). The GGUF model uses the app-private files dir, which needs no storage permission.

expo._splash_note: Expo prebuild generates android/.../values/colors.xml with splashscreen_background from the splash.backgroundColor above. Without a splash block, prebuild can emit a splashscreen.xml referencing @color/splashscreen_background while leaving that color undefined -- the exact mismatch reported. Defining it here makes the generated resources internally consistent.

ios._model_provisioning_note: UIFileSharingEnabled + LSSupportsOpeningDocumentsInPlace are the iOS equivalent of the Android adb-push flow: they let the GGUF be dropped into the app's Documents folder via Finder/Files, where modelProvisioning.ts's documentDirectory resolver (path 2, no native module needed -- the expo read gate is Android-only) finds it as Documents/models/<name>.gguf.

infoPlist.NSMicrophoneUsageDescription: Used only if you turn on live-call transcription to check for scam patterns during a call. Off by default.

extra._apiUrlNote: Base URL of the TrustTrace FastAPI backend. Read by mobile/src/config/env.ts. Overridden by EXPO_PUBLIC_TRUSTTRACE_API_URL if set. For a USB-attached physical device, run `adb reverse tcp:8000 tcp:8000` and keep localhost:8000 here -- the API then rides the same USB tunnel Metro uses, so it survives Wi-Fi/firewall issues. Over Wi-Fi instead, put your machine's LAN IP here, e.g. http://192.168.1.20:8000
